stem: A company runs a web application behind an ALB with an Auto Scaling group. During a traffic spike, new EC2 instances are launched but users still experience errors for 2 minutes until the new instances are fully initialized. What Auto Scaling feature eliminates these errors during scale-out?

options.label: A
options.text: ALB connection draining — delays instance removal during scale-in to allow in-flight requests to complete before deregistering the target.

options.label: B
options.text: EC2 instance tenancy — Dedicated Hosts launch faster

options.label: C
options.text: Warm pools — pre-initialized EC2 instances that are ready

options.label: D
options.text: Scheduled scaling policy

distractors.A: ALB connection draining — delays instance removal during scale-in to allow in-flight requests to complete before deregistering the target.

explanation: Warm pools allow you to keep pre-initialized EC2 instances in a stopped or running state. When a scale-out event occurs, instances from the warm pool enter the ASG immediately without bootstrapping delay, eliminating the 2-minute initialization window. Scheduled scaling predicts demand but doesn't pre-initialize. Dedicated Hosts don't affect boot time. Connection draining is for scale-in, not scale-out.

stem: A company runs a global multi-player gaming application. Players in South America, Europe, and Asia-Pacific all need to connect to the same game server in us-east-1 with minimal latency and consistent network performance. Which service routes player connections over AWS's global backbone rather than the public internet?

options.label: A
options.text: AWS Global Accelerator

options.label: B
options.text: VPC Peering

options.label: C
options.text: Amazon CloudFront

options.label: D
options.text: Amazon Route 53 with latency routing

explanation: AWS Global Accelerator routes traffic through AWS edge locations to AWS's private global network backbone, avoiding the unpredictable public internet. This provides consistent, low-latency networking for applications with a single endpoint in us-east-1. CloudFront is for caching static content. Route 53 latency routing resolves to the nearest endpoint but traffic still travels over the public internet. VPC Peering connects VPCs, not end users globally.

stem: A startup's application writes 10,000 records per second to DynamoDB. After 6 months, the DynamoDB table has grown to 50 TB and query performance has degraded for accessing data older than 90 days. What DynamoDB feature should they use to automatically move old items out of the table?

options.label: A
options.text: DynamoDB Streams with a Lambda function to delete old items

options.label: B
options.text: DynamoDB On-Demand capacity mode to handle the larger table size

options.label: C
options.text: DynamoDB Global Tables to distribute data across regions

options.label: D
options.text: DynamoDB Time to Live (TTL) to automatically delete items after a defined expiry timestamp

explanation: DynamoDB TTL allows you to define an attribute on each item containing an expiry timestamp. DynamoDB automatically deletes expired items in the background without consuming write capacity units. This is the correct tool for data lifecycle management in DynamoDB — cleaning up old data reduces table size and can improve query performance on active data. Streams+Lambda could work but TTL is built-in and simpler. Global Tables adds regions, not lifecycle management.

stem: A company uses Amazon RDS PostgreSQL. They need to scale write capacity beyond what a single instance can handle. Which approach supports write scaling in RDS?

options.label: A
options.text: Migrate to Amazon Aurora, which uses a distributed storage layer and can support Aurora Serverless

options.label: B
options.text: Increase the RDS instance size (vertical scaling) — this is the only option for RDS write scaling

options.label: C
options.text: Enable RDS Multi-AZ — the standby handles write scaling

options.label: D
options.text: Enable RDS Read Replicas and direct writes to them

explanation: RDS PostgreSQL has limited write scaling options — Read Replicas handle reads only. True write scaling for relational databases typically requires migrating to Aurora (which has higher write throughput) or application-level sharding. Aurora Serverless v2 can auto-scale capacity. Multi-AZ standby is for failover, not scaling — it doesn't serve writes. Vertical scaling (larger instance) helps but has hard limits.

stem: A company wants to run a data processing pipeline where Step 1 must complete before Step 2 starts, Step 2 can run two tasks in parallel, and Step 3 only runs if Step 2 tasks both succeed. Which AWS service orchestrates this workflow?

options.label: A
options.text: AWS Step Functions state machine with sequential and parallel states

options.label: B
options.text: Amazon EventBridge with multiple rules routes events to different targets but lacks native sequential state management and conditional branching for workflows.

options.label: C
options.text: Amazon SQS with multiple queues decouples components but does not orchestrate multi-step workflows with conditional logic, retries, and parallel execution.

options.label: D
options.text: AWS Lambda with recursive calls can chain functions but creates tight coupling, no visual workflow tracking, and makes error handling and timeouts difficult.

distractors.B: Amazon EventBridge with multiple rules routes events to different targets but lacks native sequential state management and conditional branching for workflows.
distractors.C: Amazon SQS with multiple queues decouples components but does not orchestrate multi-step workflows with conditional logic, retries, and parallel execution.
distractors.D: AWS Lambda with recursive calls can chain functions but creates tight coupling, no visual workflow tracking, and makes error handling and timeouts difficult.

explanation: Step Functions state machines natively support sequential states, parallel branches, error handling, and conditional branching. The workflow described (sequential → parallel → conditional) is exactly what Step Functions is built for. SQS is for message queuing, not workflow orchestration with conditional logic. EventBridge is for event routing, not stateful workflow management. Recursive Lambda calls are error-prone and lack native parallelism control.

stem: A media company stores video files in S3. They want to automatically transcode uploaded videos into multiple resolutions (1080p, 720p, 480p) whenever a new file is uploaded. Which event-driven architecture achieves this with minimal management?

options.label: A
options.text: SQS queue that stores upload notifications for batch processing nightly

options.label: B
options.text: CloudWatch Events scheduled rule that runs every hour

options.label: C
options.text: S3 Event Notification triggering an SNS topic that fans out to Lambda

options.label: D
options.text: EC2 instance that polls S3 every 5 minutes for new files

explanation: S3 Event Notifications fire immediately on object creation. Routing to SNS enables fan-out to multiple Lambda functions simultaneously — one per resolution. Lambda scales automatically per invocation. This is fully event-driven with no polling and no persistent compute. EC2 polling introduces latency and requires idle compute. CloudWatch scheduled rules are time-based, not event-based. SQS batch processing is not real-time.

stem: A company wants to migrate their on-premises Oracle database to AWS while minimizing downtime. The database must remain available during migration. Which AWS service supports continuous data replication during migration with minimal application downtime?

options.label: A
options.text: AWS Snowball Edge is a physical data transfer device designed for offline bulk migrations of terabytes to petabytes, not for continuous live database replication.

options.label: B
options.text: AWS Database Migration Service (DMS) with ongoing replication

options.label: C
options.text: AWS DataSync transfers data between on-premises storage and AWS storage services like S3 or EFS but does not support continuous database replication.

options.label: D
options.text: Amazon S3 Transfer Acceleration speeds up file uploads to S3 using CloudFront edge locations but is not applicable to database replication scenarios.

distractors.A: AWS Snowball Edge is a physical data transfer device designed for offline bulk migrations of terabytes to petabytes, not for continuous live database replication.
distractors.C: AWS DataSync transfers data between on-premises storage and AWS storage services like S3 or EFS but does not support continuous database replication.
distractors.D: Amazon S3 Transfer Acceleration speeds up file uploads to S3 using CloudFront edge locations but is not applicable to database replication scenarios.

explanation: AWS DMS supports both one-time full-load migrations and ongoing replication (change data capture). With DMS, you start a full load from the source Oracle DB to the target (RDS, Aurora, etc.), then switch to ongoing CDC replication to keep the target synchronized. During cutover, only a brief downtime (seconds to minutes) is needed. Snowball is for bulk offline data transfer. DataSync moves files, not relational database data with CDC. S3 Transfer Acceleration is for S3 uploads.

stem: A company uses Amazon ElastiCache for Redis as a cache in front of RDS. The cache hit ratio is 95%, dramatically reducing RDS load. If the ElastiCache cluster fails, the application connects directly to RDS. What does this pattern demonstrate?

options.label: A
options.text: Read-through caching — the cache always fetches from RDS on misses

options.label: B
options.text: Cache-aside pattern with graceful degradation — application handles cache miss by falling back to the database

options.label: C
options.text: Single point of failure — the cache creates a bottleneck and the architecture should use Multi-AZ ElastiCache replication to eliminate this dependency.

options.label: D
options.text: Write-through caching — all writes go to the cache first

distractors.C: Single point of failure — the cache creates a bottleneck and the architecture should use Multi-AZ ElastiCache replication to eliminate this dependency.

explanation: The cache-aside (lazy loading) pattern means the application checks the cache first. On a miss (or cache failure), it queries the database directly. This provides graceful degradation — a cache failure degrades performance but doesn't cause an outage. The RDS fallback means the cache is a performance optimization, not a hard dependency. Write-through and read-through are different caching patterns.

stem: A retail company uses an Aurora database. Their Black Friday traffic increases write load by 5x. They notice Aurora's write performance doesn't scale as quickly as their read capacity (which uses Aurora Replicas). Why is this, and what is the architectural solution?

options.label: A
options.text: Enable Aurora Multi-AZ which distributes writes across zones

options.label: B
options.text: Enable Aurora Global Database which distributes writes across regions

options.label: C
options.text: Increase the number of Aurora Replicas to handle both read and write traffic

options.label: D
options.text: Aurora replicas share storage with the primary, so reads scale easily.

explanation: Aurora's architecture uses a shared distributed storage layer — read replicas can serve reads from this shared storage without replicating data. However, all writes go to the single primary writer instance. You can scale reads by adding up to 15 replicas, but write scaling requires a larger primary instance (vertical) or application-level sharding. Aurora Multi-AZ is failover, not write scaling. Global Database has a single writer region.

stem: A company uses AWS CloudFormation to manage infrastructure. They want to ensure that the production CloudFormation stack cannot be deleted accidentally. Which CloudFormation feature prevents accidental deletion?

options.label: A
options.text: DeletionPolicy: Retain on all resources

options.label: B
options.text: Termination Protection on the stack

options.label: C
options.text: Stack Policies that deny delete actions

options.label: D
options.text: IAM permissions that restrict cloudformation:DeleteStack

explanation: CloudFormation stack Termination Protection, when enabled, prevents the stack from being deleted through the console or CLI until protection is explicitly disabled. It's a simple, direct answer to the question. DeletionPolicy: Retain keeps the underlying resources after stack deletion but doesn't prevent the deletion action. Stack Policies protect resources from unintended updates during stack updates but don't prevent stack deletion. IAM restrictions could work but Termination Protection is the CloudFormation-native feature.

stem: A company is designing a serverless API that will have highly variable traffic: near-zero most of the time, with sudden bursts to 5,000 requests per second. They are concerned about Lambda cold starts impacting user experience during bursts. What feature addresses this?

options.label: A
options.text: Lambda Reserved Concurrency to limit maximum execution count

options.label: B
options.text: Increasing Lambda memory allocation reduces cold start time

options.label: C
options.text: Lambda Provisioned Concurrency — pre-initializes execution environments to eliminate cold

options.label: D
options.text: Lambda Layers for dependency optimization

explanation: Lambda Provisioned Concurrency pre-initializes a specified number of execution environments, keeping them warm and ready to respond instantly with no initialization latency. This eliminates cold starts for the configured concurrency level. Reserved Concurrency limits (not pre-warms) concurrency. Lambda Layers reduce deployment package size. Increasing memory does slightly speed up initialization but is not a reliable cold start solution.

stem: A company deploys microservices on Amazon ECS. They need a way for services to discover each other's endpoints dynamically as instances scale and change IP. Which AWS service provides service discovery for ECS?

options.label: A
options.text: Amazon Route 53 public hosted zone handles DNS resolution for publicly accessible endpoints but does not provide service discovery within private VPC namespaces.

options.label: B
options.text: AWS Direct Connect provides private network connectivity from on-premises to AWS but is not a service discovery mechanism for ECS or microservice environments.

options.label: C
options.text: VPC Peering enables network-level connectivity between VPCs but does not provide the DNS-based service discovery layer needed for dynamic ECS service registration.

options.label: D
options.text: AWS Cloud Map — service discovery for AWS resources including ECS.

distractors.A: Amazon Route 53 public hosted zone handles DNS resolution for publicly accessible endpoints but does not provide service discovery within private VPC namespaces.
distractors.B: AWS Direct Connect provides private network connectivity from on-premises to AWS but is not a service discovery mechanism for ECS or microservice environments.
distractors.C: VPC Peering enables network-level connectivity between VPCs but does not provide the DNS-based service discovery layer needed for dynamic ECS service registration.

explanation: AWS Cloud Map provides service discovery for cloud resources. ECS integrates with Cloud Map to register service instances as they start and deregister when they stop. Services can discover each other via DNS or API calls. Route 53 public hosted zones are for internet-facing DNS. Direct Connect and VPC Peering are network connectivity solutions, not service discovery mechanisms.

stem: An e-commerce company's order processing involves: placing an order, reserving inventory, charging a credit card, and sending a confirmation email. They want to orchestrate this as a reliable workflow with automatic retries on transient failures. Which architecture is most appropriate?

options.label: A
options.text: Use SNS to chain the steps via topic notifications

options.label: B
options.text: Use a single Lambda function that performs all steps sequentially

options.label: C
options.text: Use AWS Step Functions to orchestrate Lambda functions for each step.

options.label: D
options.text: Chain Lambda functions synchronously via function calls

explanation: Step Functions provides built-in retry logic, error handling, and state tracking for each step in a workflow. If the 'charge credit card' Lambda fails transiently, Step Functions retries automatically. Each step's state is persisted. A single monolithic Lambda loses state on timeout. Chained synchronous Lambda calls can hit timeout limits and have no built-in retry coordination. SNS fan-out doesn't provide sequential orchestration with error handling.

stem: A company needs to run a containerized workload that requires access to a GPU for machine learning inference. Which Amazon ECS launch type supports GPU-enabled EC2 instances?

options.label: A
options.text: Amazon Lightsail containers — provide GPU access

options.label: B
options.text: EC2 launch type with GPU-optimized instance types (e.g., p3, g4dn)

options.label: C
options.text: AWS Fargate — supports GPU containers automatically

options.label: D
options.text: AWS Lambda — supports GPU acceleration for ML workloads

explanation: ECS with the EC2 launch type allows you to use GPU-optimized instance types (p3, g4dn, etc.) in your cluster. Containers can request GPU resources and ECS schedules them on GPU instances. Fargate does not support GPU instances. Lambda does not support GPU access. Lightsail containers don't provide GPU capability.

stem: A company's application runs in a VPC and needs to access Amazon S3 without traffic leaving the AWS network or going over the public internet. They also want to avoid NAT Gateway charges for S3 traffic. Which feature achieves both goals?

options.label: A
options.text: S3 Transfer Acceleration

options.label: B
options.text: VPC Gateway Endpoint for S3

options.label: C
options.text: VPC Peering connection to S3

options.label: D
options.text: AWS Direct Connect to S3

explanation: A VPC Gateway Endpoint for S3 routes traffic from the VPC to S3 directly through the AWS network without using a NAT Gateway, internet gateway, or leaving AWS's backbone. Gateway Endpoints are free (no per-hour or per-GB charges). NAT Gateway charges are eliminated. Traffic never traverses the public internet. VPC Peering is for VPC-to-VPC connectivity. Direct Connect is for on-premises to AWS. Transfer Acceleration uses CloudFront edge nodes.

stem: A company builds a real-time fraud detection system. Each financial transaction must be evaluated in under 50ms. The system must run 24/7 with automatic scaling based on transaction volume. What architecture best meets these requirements?

options.label: A
options.text: Batch process transactions overnight using Amazon EMR

options.label: B
options.text: SQS FIFO queue with an EC2 Auto Scaling group consuming messages

options.label: C
options.text: Amazon Redshift for real-time SQL-based fraud analysis

options.label: D
options.text: Amazon Kinesis Data Streams ingesting transactions, with Lambda consumers running the fraud.

explanation: Kinesis Data Streams with Lambda provides real-time event processing with sub-50ms latency at scale. Kinesis scales by adding shards, and Lambda scales automatically with the stream. Batch processing (EMR) doesn't provide real-time evaluation. SQS FIFO + EC2 can work but SQS has higher latency guarantees and EC2 Auto Scaling lag. Redshift is a data warehouse optimized for complex analytical queries, not low-latency transaction processing.

stem: A company uses Amazon EFS to share data between multiple EC2 instances running a content management system. As the workload grows, they notice EFS latency increasing. Which EFS performance mode should they evaluate to optimize for high throughput at the cost of slightly higher latency on metadata operations?

options.label: A
options.text: Bursting Throughput mode starts with a baseline tied to file system size and bursts higher, which is optimized for spiky workloads, not sustained high parallelism.

options.label: B
options.text: Provisioned Throughput mode sets a fixed throughput level independent of file system size, suitable for predictable high-throughput but not maximum parallel I/O.

options.label: C
options.text: General Purpose performance mode is optimized for latency-sensitive workloads with lower per-operation overhead, making it the right choice for most use cases.

options.label: D
options.text: Max I/O performance mode — scales to higher throughput

distractors.A: Bursting Throughput mode starts with a baseline tied to file system size and bursts higher, which is optimized for spiky workloads, not sustained high parallelism.
distractors.B: Provisioned Throughput mode sets a fixed throughput level independent of file system size, suitable for predictable high-throughput but not maximum parallel I/O.
distractors.C: General Purpose performance mode is optimized for latency-sensitive workloads with lower per-operation overhead, making it the right choice for most use cases.

explanation: EFS Max I/O mode supports higher throughput and operations per second for highly parallelized workloads accessing EFS from many instances. It trades slightly higher latencies on file metadata operations for greater aggregate throughput. General Purpose is better for latency-sensitive apps with a smaller scale. Bursting and Provisioned are throughput modes (different dimension from performance modes).

stem: A company wants to use Amazon Rekognition to automatically tag products in images uploaded to their e-commerce platform. The images are stored in S3. What is the correct invocation pattern?

options.label: A
options.text: Use AWS Glue to batch-process images through Rekognition on a schedule

options.label: B
options.text: Install Rekognition software on EC2 instances to process images locally

options.label: C
options.text: Configure S3 to automatically send images to Rekognition via S3 Inventory

options.label: D
options.text: Call the Rekognition API from a Lambda function triggered by S3 object creation events,

explanation: Rekognition is a fully managed API service. The standard pattern is: S3 Event Notification (on PutObject) → Lambda → Rekognition API call with the S3 object reference. Rekognition reads directly from S3 and returns labels/tags. Lambda handles the orchestration. Rekognition has no client-side software to install. Glue is for ETL, not ML inference. S3 Inventory doesn't trigger ML processing.

stem: A company is deploying a three-tier architecture: web tier (public), application tier (private), database tier (private). The application tier needs outbound internet access to call a third-party API, but should not be directly reachable from the internet. What provides outbound-only internet connectivity for private subnets?

options.label: A
options.text: Elastic IP address on the application server

options.label: B
options.text: Internet Gateway attached to the private subnet

options.label: C
options.text: NAT Gateway in a public subnet with a route from the.

options.label: D
options.text: VPC Peering to a VPC with internet access

explanation: A NAT Gateway in a public subnet allows instances in private subnets to initiate outbound connections to the internet (through the NAT) while preventing inbound connections from the internet. The private subnet's route table points to the NAT Gateway for 0.0.0.0/0 traffic. An Internet Gateway on a private subnet would expose instances publicly. VPC Peering connects VPCs. Elastic IP makes the instance publicly reachable.

stem: A company's DBA notices that a Lambda function is opening a new RDS connection on every invocation, causing the database to run out of connections during high load (thousands of concurrent Lambdas). What is the recommended solution?

options.label: A
options.text: Reduce Lambda concurrency to limit the number of simultaneous connections

options.label: B
options.text: Use Amazon RDS Proxy — it pools and multiplexes database connections.

options.label: C
options.text: Switch from RDS to DynamoDB to avoid connection limits

options.label: D
options.text: Increase the RDS instance size to support more connections

explanation: RDS Proxy sits between Lambda and RDS, maintaining a connection pool. Thousands of Lambda invocations share a much smaller pool of actual database connections. This solves the Lambda-RDS connection exhaustion problem without limiting concurrency or changing database engines. Increasing instance size helps marginally but doesn't solve the fundamental connection management problem. Reducing concurrency degrades performance.

stem: A company runs microservices on Amazon EKS. They want to manage secrets (API keys, database passwords) securely for their Kubernetes pods without storing secrets in environment variables or container images. Which AWS service integrates with EKS for this?

options.label: A
options.text: AWS Systems Manager Parameter Store or Secrets Manager via the AWS Secrets and Config Provider

options.label: B
options.text: AWS KMS — encrypt secrets in etcd

options.label: C
options.text: Amazon S3 — store secrets in an encrypted S3 bucket and mount in pods

options.label: D
options.text: AWS IAM — embed secrets in IAM policies attached to the pod's service account

explanation: The AWS Secrets and Config Provider (ASCP) with the Secrets Store CSI Driver allows Kubernetes pods to mount secrets from AWS Secrets Manager or Parameter Store as volumes or environment variables — secrets never touch the container image or Kubernetes secrets (etcd). KMS can encrypt etcd but doesn't inject secrets into pods. S3 file mounting is not native Kubernetes secret management. IAM policies cannot store application secrets.

stem: A company uses Amazon CloudWatch to monitor their application. They want to automatically recover an EC2 instance if it fails an EC2 system status check (underlying hardware issue). What CloudWatch feature enables automated recovery?

options.label: A
options.text: CloudWatch Logs captures log data for review after an event but cannot automatically trigger EC2 instance recovery the way an EC2 recovery alarm action can.

options.label: B
options.text: CloudWatch Alarm with an EC2 recovery action — automatically recovers the instance to the same host or a new host with the same instance ID and metadata.

options.label: C
options.text: CloudWatch Contributor Insights analyzes log patterns to identify top contributors to issues but does not trigger any automated remediation or recovery action.

options.label: D
options.text: A CloudWatch Events scheduled rule can restart instances on a schedule but cannot detect and respond to individual instance status check failures in real time.

distractors.A: CloudWatch Logs captures log data for review after an event but cannot automatically trigger EC2 instance recovery the way an EC2 recovery alarm action can.
distractors.C: CloudWatch Contributor Insights analyzes log patterns to identify top contributors to issues but does not trigger any automated remediation or recovery action.
distractors.D: A CloudWatch Events scheduled rule can restart instances on a schedule but cannot detect and respond to individual instance status check failures in real time.

explanation: CloudWatch supports EC2 recovery actions on alarms that monitor StatusCheckFailed_System. When triggered, EC2 performs an instance recovery — migrating the instance to healthy hardware while preserving the instance ID, private IP, Elastic IP, and EBS volumes. This is different from termination and relaunch (which loses the instance ID and private IP). Scheduled restarts are not event-driven. Contributor Insights and Logs are analytical tools.

stem: A company stores 100 TB of rarely accessed data in Amazon S3 Standard. A cost review reveals they are paying full S3 Standard prices for data accessed less than once per year. Which S3 storage class change would most reduce cost for this specific access pattern?

options.label: A
options.text: S3 One Zone-IA stores data in a single Availability Zone at lower cost than Standard-IA, but does not provide the low retrieval cost of Glacier for archives.

options.label: B
options.text: S3 Standard-IA is designed for infrequent but rapidly accessible data and has higher storage and retrieval costs than Glacier for long-term archival.

options.label: C
options.text: S3 Intelligent-Tiering automatically moves objects between access tiers based on usage patterns but carries a monitoring fee and is not optimized for pure archival.

options.label: D
options.text: S3 Glacier Flexible Retrieval (formerly S3 Glacier)

distractors.A: S3 One Zone-IA stores data in a single Availability Zone at lower cost than Standard-IA, but does not provide the low retrieval cost of Glacier for archives.
distractors.B: S3 Standard-IA is designed for infrequent but rapidly accessible data and has higher storage and retrieval costs than Glacier for long-term archival.
distractors.C: S3 Intelligent-Tiering automatically moves objects between access tiers based on usage patterns but carries a monitoring fee and is not optimized for pure archival.

explanation: For data accessed less than once per year, S3 Glacier Flexible Retrieval provides the lowest storage cost — approximately 1/10th of S3 Standard pricing. Retrieval times are minutes to hours. Standard-IA and One Zone-IA are designed for monthly access patterns and have per-retrieval fees that become expensive for very infrequent access. Intelligent-Tiering is optimal for data with unknown or changing patterns — for truly archival data (< yearly access), Glacier is cheaper.

stem: A company runs an online ticketing system where two users try to purchase the last available ticket simultaneously. Without proper handling, both could succeed, resulting in overselling. What DynamoDB feature prevents this race condition?

options.label: A
options.text: DynamoDB DAX caching

options.label: B
options.text: DynamoDB Streams to process purchases sequentially

options.label: C
options.text: DynamoDB conditional writes — update the item only

options.label: D
options.text: DynamoDB Global Tables for consistent writes across regions

explanation: DynamoDB conditional writes allow you to specify a condition that must be true for the write to succeed. For the last ticket, a condition like `available_tickets > 0` ensures the decrement only succeeds if tickets remain. The first writer succeeds; the second writer's condition fails with ConditionalCheckFailedException. This is atomic without needing distributed locks. DAX is a read cache. Streams are for event processing. Global Tables replicate data.

stem: A company uses AWS Elastic Beanstalk to deploy their application. They want to deploy a new version to 25% of instances first, monitor for errors, then gradually roll out to the rest — and be able to quickly revert if issues arise. Which Elastic Beanstalk deployment policy supports this?

options.label: A
options.text: Rolling — deploys in batches across all instances

options.label: B
options.text: All at once — deploys to all instances simultaneously

options.label: C
options.text: Immutable or Traffic-splitting — deploys new version to a separate fleet and

options.label: D
options.text: Rolling with additional batch — adds new instances during deployment

explanation: Traffic-splitting deployment (also called canary deployment in Beanstalk) sends a percentage of traffic to a new version while keeping the old version running. Immutable deployments launch a completely new set of instances. Both support quick rollback. All-at-once has no rollback without redeployment. Rolling updates the same instances in batches (slower rollback). Traffic-splitting most directly matches the 'deploy to 25% first, then gradually roll out' requirement.

stem: A company wants to host a static website with global delivery, HTTPS support, and no server management. All content is HTML/CSS/JS stored in S3. Which architecture achieves this with minimal cost and no server management?

options.label: A
options.text: S3 static website hosting with CloudFront distribution and ACM certificate

options.label: B
options.text: EC2 instances in multiple Regions with Route 53 geolocation routing

options.label: C
options.text: Amazon Lightsail with a static site template

options.label: D
options.text: Elastic Beanstalk Node.js application serving static files

explanation: S3 static website hosting + CloudFront + ACM is the canonical serverless static site pattern: no servers, global CDN delivery, free ACM certificate, HTTPS termination at CloudFront edge, and costs only for storage and CDN data transfer. EC2 multi-region has high overhead and cost. Elastic Beanstalk runs application servers unnecessarily. Lightsail involves VMs.

stem: A company analyzes user behavior data stored in S3. Currently they use Amazon EMR with Spark for batch analytics jobs that run for 4 hours daily. The cluster is idle 20 hours per day. What architecture reduces cost while maintaining the analytical capability?

options.label: A
options.text: Switch to Amazon Redshift and load data from S3

options.label: B
options.text: Enable EMR auto-scaling to scale down to 0 nodes during idle periods

options.label: C
options.text: Use Amazon Athena for ad-hoc queries and run EMR clusters only when needed,

options.label: D
options.text: Use Reserved EMR instances to reduce the idle cost

explanation: Terminating the EMR cluster after each job (using transient clusters) eliminates the 20 hours of idle cost. For ad-hoc analysis, Athena queries S3 directly with no cluster management and charges per TB scanned. EMR transient clusters can be launched on-demand for intensive Spark jobs. Reserved instances and auto-scaling both have minimum costs even when idle. Redshift requires loading data and continuous cluster costs.

stem: A company's analytics team needs to join data from S3, DynamoDB, and an RDS database in a single SQL query without moving the data. Which AWS service enables this federated query capability?

options.label: A
options.text: Amazon Redshift Spectrum — queries only S3 data from Redshift

options.label: B
options.text: Amazon Athena Federated Query — can query data across multiple sources including S3,

options.label: C
options.text: AWS Data Pipeline — moves data between sources for unified querying

options.label: D
options.text: Amazon Glue — creates a unified data catalog

explanation: Athena Federated Query uses data source connectors to query data in S3, DynamoDB, RDS, and other sources simultaneously in a single SQL query without ETL or data movement. Results are joined at query time. Glue provides the catalog (schema definitions) but doesn't run federated queries itself. Redshift Spectrum queries S3 from Redshift but doesn't span DynamoDB and RDS natively. Data Pipeline moves data, requiring extract-load cycles.

stem: Which TWO statements correctly describe differences between Amazon SQS Standard and SQS FIFO queues?

options.label: A
options.text: SQS FIFO supports higher throughput than Standard (up to 30,000 messages/second with high throughput mode vs. SQS Standard's 3,000/second)

options.label: B
options.text: SQS FIFO queues are better suited for workloads where message ordering and deduplication are critical, such as financial transactions

options.label: C
options.text: SQS Standard provides at-least-once delivery and best-effort ordering; FIFO provides exactly-once processing and strict message ordering

options.label: D
options.text: SQS Standard guarantees messages are delivered in the order they were sent

explanation: SQS Standard: nearly unlimited throughput, at-least-once delivery (rare duplicates possible), best-effort ordering. SQS FIFO: exactly-once processing, strict FIFO ordering per message group, but lower throughput (3,000/sec standard, 30,000/sec in high throughput mode). FIFO is ideal for order-sensitive workloads like financial transactions. Standard has much higher throughput than FIFO, not the reverse. Standard does NOT guarantee ordering.

stem: A company uses AWS Direct Connect for a dedicated 1 Gbps connection between their data center and AWS. They also have a Site-to-Site VPN as backup. During a Direct Connect circuit failure, traffic should automatically fail over to VPN. What AWS service provides this redundancy and automatic failover?

options.label: A
options.text: VPC Peering requires individual peering connections between each pair of VPCs and does not support routing between on-premises networks and multiple VPCs.

options.label: B
options.text: AWS Transit Gateway with both Direct Connect and VPN attachments

options.label: C
options.text: Amazon Route 53 with health checks provides DNS failover and traffic routing but does not aggregate and route network connectivity from multiple hybrid connections.

options.label: D
options.text: AWS CloudFront serves as a content delivery network for HTTP/HTTPS traffic and is not applicable to hybrid network routing or Direct Connect failover.

distractors.A: VPC Peering requires individual peering connections between each pair of VPCs and does not support routing between on-premises networks and multiple VPCs.
distractors.C: Amazon Route 53 with health checks provides DNS failover and traffic routing but does not aggregate and route network connectivity from multiple hybrid connections.
distractors.D: AWS CloudFront serves as a content delivery network for HTTP/HTTPS traffic and is not applicable to hybrid network routing or Direct Connect failover.

explanation: AWS Transit Gateway can attach both Direct Connect (via Direct Connect Gateway) and Site-to-Site VPN connections. BGP routing priorities can be set so Direct Connect is preferred, with VPN as automatic failover. When Direct Connect fails, BGP routes traffic through the VPN attachment automatically. Route 53 health checks work for DNS failover, not network-layer failover. VPC Peering doesn't apply to on-premises connectivity. CloudFront is a CDN, not a network failover mechanism.
